name: FIFA Corruption Full
description: The theory of 'FIFA Corruption Full' encompasses widespread and systemic corruption within the Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA), the international governing body of association football. Proponents argue that this corruption extends beyond isolated incidents to involve bribery, vote-buying, match-fixing, and embezzlement at the highest levels of the organization, influencing World Cup hosting bids, player transfers, and broadcasting rights. The theory gained significant traction with the 2015 FIFA corruption case, which led to indictments of numerous high-ranking officials. Evidence cited includes leaked documents, whistleblower testimonies, and financial irregularities uncovered by journalists and investigative agencies. The mainstream perspective acknowledges that corruption has occurred within FIFA, as evidenced by the indictments and convictions. However, some argue that characterizing it as 'full' or 'complete' is an oversimplification, suggesting that reforms and oversight mechanisms are gradually mitigating the problem, though challenges remain. Critics emphasize the difficulty of proving systemic corruption across all levels and activities of FIFA, and suggest that focusing solely on corruption overlooks other factors influencing the organization's decisions. The theory is fueled by a general distrust of large, opaque organizations and the perception that money and power corrupt individuals and institutions.
subtitle: Systemic corruption within world football's governing body.

claims.claim: FIFA officials routinely accept bribes for World Cup hosting votes.
claims.evidence: Allegations and evidence presented in the 2015 FIFA scandal, including testimonies and financial records.
claims.criticism: While evidence of bribery in specific cases exists, proving it is routine and affects every vote is difficult. Reforms have been implemented to make the bidding process more transparent.

claims.claim: Match-fixing is rampant in international football, orchestrated by criminal organizations with FIFA officials turning a blind eye.
claims.evidence: Reports of suspicious betting patterns and investigations into specific matches, linking them to organized crime.
claims.criticism: While match-fixing is a real problem, its extent is debated. FIFA has implemented integrity programs and works with law enforcement to combat it, though challenges persist in proving collusion within FIFA itself.

claims.claim: FIFA officials embezzle funds intended for football development projects in developing nations.
claims.evidence: Audits revealing misused funds and discrepancies in financial reports related to development programs.
claims.criticism: FIFA acknowledges past shortcomings in oversight but claims to have strengthened its internal controls and increased transparency in development spending. Independent audits are now more frequent.

keyFigures.name: Sepp Blatter
keyFigures.role: Former FIFA President, implicated in numerous corruption allegations.

keyFigures.name: Jack Warner
keyFigures.role: Former FIFA Vice-President, indicted on corruption charges.

keyFigures.name: Andrew Jennings
keyFigures.role: Investigative journalist who exposed FIFA corruption for decades.

mythologyConnections: The concept of 'greed corrupts' found in many myths
The idea of hidden power structures influencing events, similar to secret societies in mythology

timeline.event: João Havelange becomes FIFA President, marking a period of rapid commercialization.
Controversial World Cup hosting decisions for Russia (2018) and Qatar (2022).
2015 FIFA corruption case leads to indictments of numerous officials.
Gianni Infantino becomes FIFA President, promising reforms.
